transcript.text: what does this project do / who uses it?

Backend service for a tiered avatar personalization product. Core tier renders the avatar and handles sessions with no personal-data handling at all. An opt-in analytics tier adds usage tracking. Used by our own product teams, not sold directly to customers.
I see feature:analytics-tier and feature:personalization-tier flags in src/features.ts — which of these are actually built and reachable today?
analytics-tier is live in production. personalization-tier is flagged but the code behind it doesn't exist yet — we're still designing the preference-profile schema.
does the analytics tier's usage-event store register with the consent registry in src/consent/registry.ts the same way the user table does?
Good catch — no, actually. It should, but whoever added eventStore.ts a few months back didn't wire it in. That's a real gap, not intentional.

GDPR export/erasure applies once the analytics tier is on, since that's the first tier that stores personal data broadly enough to trigger it. The core tier alone has no personal-data store, so nothing applies until analytics-tier is enabled.
what coverage percentage should gate-6 enforce?
80%, same as our other TS services.

compliance.note: Analytics tier is the first tier that stores personal data broadly enough to trigger export/erasure obligations — already live, so this requirement is enforced now, not forward-declared
personalization-tier is flagged in src/features.ts but not yet built — tracked so the team sees GDPR scope expanding the day it ships, not enforced until then

gateScripts.gate-1-supply-chain:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
if [ -f package.json ]; then
  audit_json="$(npm audit --omit=dev --json 2>/dev/null || true)"
  vulns="$(echo "$audit_json" | node -e "let d='';process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on('end',()=>{try{const j=JSON.parse(d);console.log(j.metadata?.vulnerabilities?.total||0)}catch(e){console.log(0)}})")"
  if [ "$vulns" -gt 0 ]; then
    issues=$vulns
    out="package.json:1\t$vulns known vulnerable dependencies via npm audit"$'\n'
  fi
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-2-completeness:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
while IFS= read -r hit; do
  [ -z "$hit" ] && continue
  out+="$hit\tstub marker or not-implemented throw"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(grep -rnE 'TODO|FIXME|not implemented' src/ 2>/dev/null | cut -d: -f1,2 || true)
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-3-security:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
node tools/scan-security-rules.mjs src/ --format=gate >> /tmp/gate3.$$ 2>/dev/null || true
while IFS= read -r line; do
  [ -z "$line" ] && continue
  out+="$line"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < /tmp/gate3.$$ 2>/dev/null || true
rm -f /tmp/gate3.$$
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; echo "ZERO-TOLERANCE"; fi
[ "$issues" -eq 0 ] && exit 0 || exit 1
gateScripts.gate-4-docs-parity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
if ! grep -q 'consent/registry.ts' README.md 2>/dev/null; then
  out+="README.md:1\tdata-subject-request section drifted from src/consent/registry.ts\n"
  issues=$((issues+1))
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-5-compliance:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
# compliance-overclaim: every named reference in SECURITY.md's Controls
# section must resolve to a real file in the repo.
while IFS=$'\t' read -r line ref; do
  [ -z "$ref" ] && continue
  if [ ! -f "$ref" ]; then
    out+="SECURITY.md:$line\tcompliance-overclaim: names $ref as backing evidence but that file does not exist\n"
    issues=$((issues+1))
  fi
done < <(node tools/scan-compliance-claims.mjs SECURITY.md)
# unregistered-personal-data-store: every store-shaped file under
# src/datastores/ must call register() from src/consent/registry.ts.
while IFS=$'\t' read -r line file; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  out+="$file:$line\tunregistered-personal-data-store: persists personal data keyed by user but never registers with src/consent/registry.ts\n"
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(node tools/scan-personal-data-stores.mjs src/datastores/ src/consent/registry.ts)
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-6-test-integrity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
result=$(npx vitest run --coverage 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$result" | grep -q 'FAIL'; then
  out+="test/registry.test.ts:1\tvitest reported a failing test\n"
  issues=$((issues+1))
fi
pct=$(echo "$result" | grep -oE 'All files[^|]*\|[^|]*' | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
if [ -n "$pct" ] && awk "BEGIN{exit !($pct < 80)}"; then
  out+="coverage/lcov.info:1\tcoverage ${pct}% below 80% threshold\n"
  issues=$((issues+1))
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-7-agent-context:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
if [ ! -f CLAUDE.md ]; then
  echo -e "CLAUDE.md\tmissing agent context file at repo root"
  issues=$((issues+1))
fi
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
